evidence: The paper uses permutation importance: "The relative importance of predictor variables was estimated by permuting predictor variable values across records, rerunning the model, and measuring the decrease in predictive accuracy." The impurity-importance bias only applies to MDI/Gini importance, not permutation importance. Variable ranking and magnitude are corroborated with SHAP: "Feature contributions (i.e., SHAP values) convey relative variable importance for individual predictions and illustrate both the magnitude and direction." The ranking is central to conclusions about drivers: "Carbonate geology, impervious cover, forest cover, and snow depth were the most important variables" and "Urbanization and deicer applications in snowier regions were the leading drivers."
